varieties.name: Eucalyptus tereticornis
varieties.origin: Endemic to eastern Australia, widespread from Sydney to far north Queensland, 1700s
varieties.traits: Tall tree 30-50m, smooth white/grey bark, open habit, fast growth 1-2m/year
varieties.availability: Widely available in Queensland nurseries, humid subtropical climates City Council plantings

varieties.name: Eucalyptus grandis
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia, NSW-Queensland coast, 1800s discovery
varieties.traits: Very tall 50-70m (up to 86m), smooth white to pink-grey bark, straight trunk
varieties.yield_notes: One of highest productivity eucalypts globally, fast growth rate
varieties.availability: Common in Queensland nurseries, forestry plantations

varieties.name: Eucalyptus saligna
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia, Sydney to Maryborough Queensland, named 1797
varieties.traits: Tall 30-55m (rarely 65m), smooth pale grey/white bark, rough brownish base
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity, excellent timber quality
varieties.availability: Queensland native nurseries, forestry sources

varieties.name: Eucalyptus robusta
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia coast, Rockhampton Queensland to NSW, named 1795
varieties.traits: Tree 20-30m, thick spongy reddish-brown bark, dense dark green canopy
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity on swampy sites, excellent coppicing
varieties.availability: Queensland wetland restoration nurseries, native specialists

varieties.name: Eucalyptus pilularis
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia, Eden NSW to SE Queensland, historical timber species
varieties.traits: Very tall 60-70m, rough fibrous grey bark on lower trunk, smooth white above
varieties.yield_notes: Very high timber productivity, valuable commercial species
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry nurseries, Redland Shire conservation programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus microcorys
varieties.origin: NSW-Queensland coast, Gosford to Hervey Bay including Fraser Island
varieties.traits: Tree 25m+ tall by 10m wide, fibrous bark, excellent shade tree
varieties.availability: Queensland native nurseries, humid subtropical climates region specialists

varieties.name: Eucalyptus resinifera subsp. hemilampra
varieties.origin: Southern coast NSW to Queensland, Taree to Gladstone, historical timber
varieties.traits: Tall 40m+, reddish-brown stringy fibrous bark with resin
varieties.availability: Queensland conservation nurseries, native plant specialists

varieties.name: Eucalyptus siderophloia
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia, Maryborough/Springsure Queensland to Sydney, 1867 description
varieties.traits: Tree 20-45m, hard dark furrowed grey/black ironbark, diamond-shaped buds
varieties.availability: Queensland native nurseries, ironbark specialists

varieties.name: Eucalyptus dunnii
varieties.origin: Restricted eastern Australia, Dorrigo/Coffs Harbour to Warwick/Canungra Queensland, 1905
varieties.traits: Tall 50m, rough grey/brown corky base, smooth white/cream bark above
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity in suitable sites, fast growth
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry nurseries, plantation suppliers

varieties.name: Corymbia citriodora
varieties.origin: Queensland, Coffs Harbour to Cooktown/Cape York, temperate-tropical zones
varieties.traits: Tall 20-40m, smooth white to pink/coppery bark, strongly lemon-scented leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Rapid growth up to 2m/year, excellent timber and oil
varieties.availability: Widely available Queensland nurseries, humid subtropical climates landscape suppliers

varieties.name: Corymbia maculata
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia, Bega NSW to Taree, extending north to humid subtropical climates area
varieties.traits: Tall 45-60m, smooth mottled pinkish-grey/bluish-grey bark, dimpled appearance
varieties.yield_notes: Very high productivity, premium timber species
varieties.availability: Queensland native nurseries, humid subtropical climates region suppliers

varieties.name: Corymbia ficifolia (grafted on C. maculata)
varieties.origin: Western Australia native, grafted for Queensland cultivation
varieties.traits: Small-medium tree, spectacular red/pink/orange flowers, silvery-blue foliage
varieties.availability: Neilson's Native Nursery humid subtropical climates, specialist Queensland grafted suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus camaldulensis subsp. camaldulensis
varieties.origin: Widest distribution of any eucalypt - across mainland Australia, 1832 description
varieties.traits: Tree 20-45m, smooth white/cream bark with colored patches, fast growth
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity, excellent coppicing, up to 2m/year growth
varieties.availability: Widely available Queensland nurseries, revegetation suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus cloeziana
varieties.origin: Wide distribution Queensland - coastal high rainfall and inland dry areas
varieties.traits: Medium-tall tree, persistent rough bark, high genetic variability between provenances
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent productivity, priority species for subtropical hardwood plantings
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry nurseries, Gympie region suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus argophloia
varieties.origin: Restricted to near Chinchilla, southern inland Queensland
varieties.traits: Medium tree, smooth white bark, adapted to subhumid conditions
varieties.availability: Queensland forestry research stations, specialist suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus kabiana
varieties.origin: Very restricted - Mt Beerwah area, SE Queensland
varieties.traits: Small mallee 6m in 7 years, stays compact, koala-friendly
varieties.yield_notes: Low timber yield but high conservation value
varieties.availability: Moreton Bay Regional Council, Pine Rivers Koala Care Association, SE Queensland conservation nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus tereticornis (Mysore Gum)
varieties.origin: Australian native, introduced to India 1790s, Mysore region selection
varieties.traits: Tree to 25m in India, smooth white bark, fast growing
varieties.yield_notes: Most widely planted eucalypt in India, excellent biomass
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Forest Department, private nurseries throughout tropical monsoon climates, tropical monsoon climates region

varieties.name: Eucalyptus grandis
varieties.origin: Australian native, introduced to India for pulp industry
varieties.traits: Tall tree to 50m, straight white trunk, smooth bark
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Forest Research Institute Peechi, forestry programs

varieties.name: Eucalyptus globulus
varieties.origin: Tasmania/Victoria Australia, first introduced India 1843 in Nilgiris
varieties.traits: Tree to 30m in India, smooth white bark, glaucous juvenile leaves
varieties.availability: Hill station nurseries in tropical monsoon climates, Munnar/Idukki region

varieties.name: Eucalyptus camaldulensis
varieties.origin: Widest natural eucalypt distribution in Australia, introduced India 1850s
varieties.traits: Tree 15-25m in India, smooth mottled white bark, fast growth
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates agricultural nurseries, agroforestry suppliers

varieties.name: Corymbia citriodora
varieties.origin: Queensland Australia, introduced India for essential oil
varieties.traits: Tall tree, smooth pink-white bark, strong lemon-scented foliage
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates essential oil grower cooperatives, agroforestry nurseries

varieties.name: Eucalyptus robusta
varieties.origin: NSW-Queensland Australia, introduced to India for wet sites
varieties.traits: Tree 20-30m, thick spongy fibrous bark, tolerates waterlogging
varieties.availability: Wetland restoration projects tropical monsoon climates, Kuttanad region specialists

varieties.name: Eucalyptus saligna
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia, widely planted India pre-1918 as 'E. saligna'
varieties.traits: Tall tree, smooth bark, discolorous leaves
varieties.availability: Limited in tropical monsoon climates, forestry research stations

varieties.name: Eucalyptus hybrid (E. tereticornis form)
varieties.origin: Selected form of E. tereticornis, developed in Mysore region, India
varieties.traits: Fast-growing, superior growth to parent species, overtops weeds
varieties.yield_notes: Highest biomass of Indian eucalypts, 2.5-5 tons/ha/year
varieties.availability: Widely available - tropical monsoon climates Forest Department, all tropical monsoon climates district nurseries, tropical monsoon climates agricultural suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus urophylla
varieties.origin: Timor and islands NW of Australia, introduced India
varieties.traits: Medium tree, adapted to tropical highlands
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates forestry research programs, limited commercial availability

varieties.name: Eucalyptus pellita
varieties.origin: Coastal North Queensland and New Guinea
varieties.traits: Tree with persistent rough bark, tropical adaptation
varieties.availability: Research stations, specialized tropical forestry suppliers

varieties.name: Eucalyptus crebra
varieties.origin: Eastern Australia, introduced India 1790s by Tipu Sultan
varieties.traits: Ironbark species, hard deeply furrowed bark
varieties.availability: Limited, forestry trials
